description: Deterministic AHK v2 micro-benchmark. Each task asks a model for an AutoHotkey v2 function named Solve() that returns a single value. A driver prints String(Solve()) to stdout; the harness runs it via CloudAHK and compares stdout (trimmed) to `expected`. Pass/fail is fully automatic.

tasks.prompt: Build a Map from the keys ["a", "b", "c"] and the values [10, 20, 30], then return the value stored under key "b".
Return the sum of all integers from 1 to 100 inclusive.
Return the string "AutoHotkey" with its characters in reverse order.
Create a counter closure: a function that returns an incrementing integer, starting at 1 on its first call. Call it three times and return the value from the third call.
Using RegExMatch on the string "2026-06-05" with a pattern that captures the four-digit year, return that captured year as a string.
Return the largest value in the array [3, 7, 2, 9, 4].
Split the string "a,b,c" on commas, then join the parts back together with a hyphen and return the result (i.e. "a-b-c").
